name: database-datastore
summary: Full database-backed entity stack: datastore, table, model, adapter, and DB handler in one shot.
description: Creates a full database-backed datastore: interface, handler interface, implementation, model, adapter, table, and database handler
problem: You have a new entity that needs CRUD against a database table and you want the entire idiomatic PHPNomad stack scaffolded in one shot — typed model, table schema, adapter, datastore interface and implementation, and the database-backed handler that ties them together.
appliesWhen: You're starting a new entity that will live in the database
You want the conventional PHPNomad data layer end-to-end with no gaps
You have a stable schema in mind for the entity
You want one command that produces a working baseline you can flesh out
avoidWhen: You only need part of the stack — use the individual recipes instead
The data is non-relational or session-scoped
You're integrating with an existing schema you don't control
You already have a datastore and just need to swap storage — use phpnomad/database-handler alone
examples: Set up everything I need to store orders in the database
Scaffold a full data layer for tracking subscriptions
Bootstrap CRUD for payouts with a database table
Create the entire data stack for a new entity
tradeoffs: Generates seven files plus registrations in one command. Fast to bootstrap, but you'll be editing a lot of TODOs. Use the smaller recipes when you only need part of the stack.
outputs: A {name}Datastore class plus its public and handler interfaces
DI bindings tying it all together
postApply: Fill in columns on the {name}sTable schema
Run migrations to create the actual table
Smoke test reads and writes through the datastore

name.aiHint: Singular PascalCase. The shared name used across all the generated files.

tableName.aiHint: snake_case, plural. The actual SQL table name.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register DI bindings in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that owns related data-layer bindings. If unsure, use the project's root initializer.
